name: Income Care Unit™
description_short: Financial consulting firm specializing in rental property owners' bookkeeping, tax planning, and cashflow management across U.S. and international portfolios.
description_long:
Income Care Unit™ is a financial consulting firm founded by Candace that focuses exclusively on rental property owners—from first-time buyers to experienced portfolio managers. The firm emerged from the founder's personal experience navigating homeownership and rental income management, initially sharing strategies on Instagram before formalizing into a consulting practice. The company positions itself as a "primary financial care provider for rentals," emphasizing personalized, holistic financial guidance rather than transactional services.

The firm offers a comprehensive suite of services including bookkeeping, cashflow management, business fundability analysis, payroll services, U.S. and international tax advisory, financial planning, strategic referral partnerships, and coaching/consulting. They serve three distinct client segments: aspiring rentalpreneurs (first-time buyers), active U.S. rental owners, and global rentalpreneurs with international properties. Services address the integrated nature of rental business finances, treating personal and business credit, budgeting, and tax planning as interconnected systems rather than separate concerns.

Income Care Unit distinguishes itself through its rental-specific focus and mindset-oriented approach. Rather than purely transactional bookkeeping, they emphasize psychological and strategic elements: helping clients "stop reacting emotionally to money," think like business owners, and align financial decisions with personal goals. They offer proactive tax planning (pre-purchase, ongoing, and expansion phases) and maintain physical/virtual presence in New York, Florida, and Kenya, enabling service to both domestic and international rental investors. Client testimonials highlight the coaching and behavioral finance components alongside technical services.

Honestly, Income Care Unit is a boutique consulting firm rather than a regulated lender or credit repair service. They appear to operate as a financial advisory/bookkeeping practice, not a consumer finance product offering. The website emphasizes high-touch personalized service, which implies premium pricing and limited scalability. No pricing is disclosed, no loan products are offered, and client acquisition appears relationship-driven. This is a specialized professional services firm for a niche market (rental property owners), not a traditional consumer finance alternative for general credit or lending needs.
address: 30 Broad St 14th floor, New York, NY 10004

company_info.headquarters: 30 Broad St 14th floor, New York, NY 10004

pros: Specialized expertise in rental property financial management—addresses the unique integration of personal and business finances for landlords
Holistic approach combining bookkeeping, tax planning, cashflow management, and behavioral coaching in one service model
Proactive tax planning at multiple lifecycle stages (pre-purchase, ongoing, expansion) rather than reactive year-end filing
International rental portfolio support with U.S. tax compliance coordination—rare service for global investors
Founder-led consulting with personal rental property ownership experience, ensuring credibility and practical understanding
Virtual appointment availability alongside physical office locations in New York and Florida
Emphasis on financial mindset and behavioral change alongside technical bookkeeping, per client testimonials
cons: No pricing transparency on website—clients must contact for quotes, creating friction in decision-making
Boutique service model with limited scalability; appears to rely on founder/small team, raising capacity questions
Not a regulated lending or credit repair entity—cannot dispute credit errors, issue loans, or provide traditional credit services
High-touch personalized approach likely means premium pricing, potentially inaccessible to budget-conscious rental owners
Limited online reviews or third-party verification of service quality visible on website; credibility relies primarily on founder narrative
best_for: First-time rental property buyers preparing for underwriting and seeking mindset and credit alignment before purchase
Active U.S. rental owners with multiple properties seeking integrated bookkeeping, tax, and cashflow optimization
International real estate investors managing properties across borders who need U.S. tax compliance and cross-border financial coordination
diagnosis: Income Care Unit is best for rental property owners—aspiring or active—seeking integrated financial guidance that combines technical bookkeeping with behavioral coaching and proactive tax planning. The main caveat is that this is a premium boutique consulting firm, not a consumer lending or credit repair service; it's designed for entrepreneurs building rental portfolios, not general consumers seeking emergency loans, credit fixes, or traditional banking products.
services: Business fundability analysis (credit and lending preparation)
Pre-purchase tax planning for rental investments
Strategic referral partnerships to complementary service providers
